docs.title: Home
docs.text:
What is Rulebook?

  • Library extension A set of additional rules for static code analysis tools
  • Multiple languages Originally written for Kotlin, it now supports Java, Groovy and Python

How does it work?

flowchart LR
  subgraph "Project"
    src(Source code)
    config[Config files]
  end
  subgraph "Static analysis"
    linters[Linter libraries]
    extensions[Rule extensions]
  end
  subgraph "Report"
    cli[Build failure<br>on lint errors]
    ide[Live warnings]
  end
  subgraph "Refactor"
    manual(Manual edits<br>using suggestions)
  end
  src -- run --> linters
  linters -- CLI --> cli
  linters -- IDE --> ide
  cli -- fix --> manual
  ide -- fix --> manual

docs.text:
• : The rule is fully implemented.
  • : Functionality already exists in other rules.
  • : The rule is already supported by the linter.
  • : Not supported due to technical limitations.
  • : Feature currently unexplored.
  • Empty: not applicable to this language.

docs.title: Integration
docs.text:
The main linter libraries can be downloaded using package managers. Preferrably, use IDE plugins to instantly see the issues while coding.

Integrate the linter tools

  • Java

    Checkstyle is a development tool to help programmers write Java code that adheres to a coding standard.

    Maven Plugin Gradle Plugin IDEA plugin

  • Groovy

    CodeNarc is similar to popular static analysis tools such as PMD or Checkstyle.

    Maven Plugin Gradle Plugin IDEA plugin

  • Kotlin

    An anti-bikeshedding Kotlin linter with built-in formatter.

    Gradle Plugin (Unofficial) IDEA plugin

  • C C++

    A tool for static C/C++ code analysis.

    IDEA plugin

  • Python

    It's not just a linter that annoys you!

    IDEA plugin

  • JavaScript TypeScript

    Find and fix problems in your JavaScript code.

    IDEA plugin

docs.title: Abstract class require abstract method
docs.text:
Abstract modifier in a class is unnecessary if the class has no abstract methods.

abstract class Message {
    fun send() {
        // implementation
    }
}

docs.title: Avoid primitive names
docs.text:
When declaring string and primitive variables, it is tempting to use simple names based on their type. In fact, they are usually recommendations given by the IDE. But these names are not descriptive and hard to understand what their purpose is.

val string = "Alice"

val int = 30

docs.title: Concise brackets, parentheses and tags
docs.text:
Brackets and parentheses without content should be wrapped in a single line.

var emptyList = [
]
var emptyMap = [
  :
]

docs.title: Delete empty file
docs.text:
Content of a file should not be empty or consist of only whitespace characters, which may be spaces, tabs or newlines.

package com.example

docs.title: Hide utility class instance
docs.text:
To prevent instantiating a utility class, put a final modifier on the class and add a private constructor.

class Strings {
    public Strings() {}

    static String capitalize(String str) {
        return str.substring(0, 1).toUpperCase() + str.substring(1);
    }
}

docs.title: Move trailing elvis to the next line
docs.text:
In a multiline statement, the elvis operator should align with chained method calls instead of the same column.

val bananas =
    fruits
        .filter { it.type == BANANA }
        .takeUnless { it.isEmpty() } ?: return

docs.title: No blank lines at initial, final and consecutive comments
docs.text:
Comment and block comment bodies should not have leading or trailing blank lines. No consecutive blank lines are allowed in the comment body.

/**
 *
 * The main function.
 *
 */
fun main() {
    // initialize the logger
    //
    //
    // and print the starting message
    val logger = Logger()
    logger.info("Starting the application")
}

docs.title: Remove negation
docs.text:
In functions that support both positive and negative conditions, the positive condition should be preferred.

assertTrue(!user.isAuthorized)

docs.title: Sort named imports
docs.text:
When importing multiple members from the same package, they should be sorted alphabetically.

from math import sin, cos

alpha = cos(0.5)
beta = sin(0.5)

docs.title: Trim multiline brackets, parentheses and tags
docs.text:
Method declaration, calls and collection initializers should not start or end with a blank line.

def getColors(

    int red,
    int green,
    int blue
) {
    return [
        red,
        green,
        blue,

    ]
}

docs.title: Use else-if
docs.text:
Single if statement in an else block should be converted to an else-if statement.

if (x > 0) {
    println("Positive")
} else {
    if (x < 0) {
        println("Negative")
    } else {
        println("Zero")
    }
}

docs.title: Use named object directly
docs.text:
Single calls in Gradle NamedDomainObjectContainer should not be wrapped in a block.

dependencies {
    implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlin:kotlin-stdlib:1.5.0")
}

docs.title: Block tag punctuation
docs.text:
Description of certain block tags, if present, should end with a period, question mark or exclamation mark.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++ 
/**
 * @param num
 * @return the new size of the group
 */
abstract int add(int num);

/**
 * @param num
 * @return the new size of the group
 */
abstract def add(int num)

/**
 * @param num
 * @return the new size of the group
 */
abstract fun add(int num): Int

/**
 * @param num
 * @return the new size of the group
 */
virtual int add(int num) = 0;

After

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++ 
/**
* @param num
* @return the new size of the group.
*/
abstract int add(int num);

/**
* @param num
* @return the new size of the group.
*/
abstract def add(int num)

/**
* @param num
* @return the new size of the group.
*/
abstract fun add(int num): Int

/**
* @param num
* @return the new size of the group.
*/
virtual int add(int num) = 0;
Configuration Setting Default value BlockTagPunctuation#tags @param, @return BlockTagPunctuation#tags @param, @return rulebook_punctuate_block_tags @constructor, @receiver, @property, @param, @return --punctuate-block-tags @param, @return

docs.title: Trailing comma in call
docs.text:
Put a trailing comma in a multiline call site, omit when it is a single line.

Before

GroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
var items =
    [
        'milks',
        'eggs'
    ] as Set

println(items,)

val items =
    setOf(
        "milks",
        "eggs"
    )

println(items,)

items = \
    set(
        'milks',
        'eggs'
    )

print(items,)

const items =
    new Set([
        'milks',
        'eggs'
    ]);

console.log(items,);

const items: Set<string> =
    new Set([
        'milks',
        'eggs'
    ]);

console.log(items,);

After

GroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
var items =
    [
        'milks',
        'eggs',
    ] as Set

println(items)

val items =
    setOf(
        "milks",
        "eggs",
    )

println(items)

items = \
    set(
        'milks',
        'eggs',
    )

print(items)

const items =
    new Set([
        'milks',
        'eggs',
    ]);

console.log(items);

const items: Set<string> =
    new Set([
        'milks',
        'eggs',
    ]);

console.log(items);

docs.title: Trailing comma in collection
docs.text:
Put a trailing comma in a multiline collection site, omit when it is a single line. In Java and Groovy, this rule applies to array initializers. In Python, this rule applies to tuples.

Before

JavaGroovyPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
int[][] ticTacToe = {
    {0, 0, 0,},
    {0, 0, 0,},
    {0, 0, 0,}
};

var ticTacToe = [
    [0, 0, 0,],
    [0, 0, 0,],
    [0, 0, 0,]
]

tic_tac_toe = (
    (0, 0, 0,),
    (0, 0, 0,),
    (0, 0, 0,)
)

const ticTacToe = [
    [0, 0, 0,],
    [0, 0, 0,],
    [0, 0, 0,]
];

const ticTacToe: number[][] = [
    [0, 0, 0,],
    [0, 0, 0,],
    [0, 0, 0,]
];

After

JavaGroovyPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
int[][] ticTacToe = {
    {0, 0, 0},
    {0, 0, 0},
    {0, 0, 0},
};

var ticTacToe = [
    [0, 0, 0],
    [0, 0, 0],
    [0, 0, 0],
]

tic_tac_toe = (
    (0, 0, 0),
    (0, 0, 0),
    (0, 0, 0),
)

const ticTacToe = [
    [0, 0, 0],
    [0, 0, 0],
    [0, 0, 0],
];

const ticTacToe: number[][] = [
    [0, 0, 0],
    [0, 0, 0],
    [0, 0, 0],
];

docs.title: Trailing comma in declaration
docs.text:
Put a trailing comma in a multiline declaration site, omit when it is a single line.

Before

KotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
fun updateInventory(item: String,) {}

fun createInventory(
    item: String,
    quantity: Int
) {}

def update_inventory(item,):
    pass


def create_inventory(
    item,
    quantity
):
    pass

function updateInventory(item,) {}

function createInventory(
    item,
    quantity
) {}

function updateInventory(item: string,): void {}

function createInventory(
    item: string,
    quantity: number
): void {}

After

KotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
fun updateInventory(item: String) {}

fun createInventory(
    item: String,
    quantity: Int,
) {}

def update_inventory(item):
    pass


def create_inventory(
    item,
    quantity,
):
    pass

function updateInventory(item) {}

function createInventory(
    item,
    quantity,
) {}

function updateInventory(item: string): void {}

function createInventory(
    item: string,
    quantity: number,
): void {}

docs.title: Unused import
docs.text:
Remove unused import statements.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
import com.example.fruit.Apple;
import com.example.fruit.Banana;

Apple apple = new Apple();

import com.example.fruit.Apple
import com.example.fruit.Banana

var apple = new Apple()

import com.example.fruit.Apple
import com.example.fruit.Banana

val apple = Apple()

from fruit import Apple, Banana

apple = Apple()

import { Apple, Banana } from 'fruit';

const apple = new Apple();

import { Apple, Banana } from 'fruit';

const apple: Apple = new Apple();

After

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
import com.example.fruit.Apple;

Apple apple = new Apple();

import com.example.fruit.Apple

var apple = new Apple()

import com.example.fruit.Apple

val apple = Apple()

from fruit import Apple

apple = Apple()

import { Apple } from 'fruit';

const apple = new Apple();

import { Apple } from 'fruit';

const apple: Apple = new Apple();

docs.title: Wildcard import
docs.text:
Import directives must be single-type instead of wildcard imports.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
import com.example.fruit.*;

List<Fruit> fruits = Arrays.asList(new Apple(), new Banana());

import com.example.fruit.*

var fruits = [new Apple(), new Banana()]

import com.example.fruit.*

val fruits = listOf(Apple(), Banana())

from fruit import *

fruits = [Apple(), Banana()]

import * as fruit from 'fruit';

const fruits = [new fruit.Apple(), new fruit.Banana()];

import * as fruit from 'fruit';

const fruits: Fruit[] = [new fruit.Apple(), new fruit.Banana()];

After

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
import com.example.fruit.Apple;
import com.example.fruit.Banana;

List<Fruit> fruits = Arrays.asList(new Apple(), new Banana());

import com.example.fruit.Apple
import com.example.fruit.Banana

var fruits = [new Apple(), new Banana()]

import com.example.fruit.Apple
import com.example.fruit.Banana

val fruits = listOf(Apple(), Banana())

from fruit import Apple, Banana

fruits = [Apple(), Banana()]

import { Apple, Banana } from 'fruit';

const fruits = [new Apple(), new Banana()];

import { Apple, Banana, Fruit } from 'fruit';

const fruits: Fruit[] = [new Apple(), new Banana()];

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
"""

After

/** The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. */

"""The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog."""

docs.title: Braces clip
docs.text:
Empty code blocks should be joined with the preceding code.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonJavaScriptTypeScript

}

def main() {
}

fun main() {
}

void main() {
}

foo = {
}

function main() {
}

function main(): void {
}

After

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
void main() {}

def main() {}

fun main() {}

void main() {}

foo = {}

function main() {}

function main(): void {}

docs.text:
Empty generic types should be joined with the preceding code.

Before

JavaGroovyC/C++ 
List<Float> points =
    new ArrayList<
        >();

    new ArrayList<
        >()

template<
> int points() {
    return 0;
}

After

    new ArrayList<>();

docs.title: Deprecated type
docs.text:
Prefer to use built-in types provided by the language.

Before

KotlinPythonTypeScript 
import java.util.ArrayList

val names = arrayListOf<String>()
val people = java.util.ArrayList<Person>()

from typing import Optional

def get_name(person) -> Optional[str]:
    return person['name']

type Nullable<T> = T | null;

function getName(person: any): Nullable<string> {
    return person.name;
}

After

KotlinPythonTypeScript 
val names = arrayListOf<String>()
val people = ArrayList<Person>()

def get_name(person) -> str | None:
    return person['name']

function getName(person: any): string | null {
    return person.name;
}

docs.title: Double quotes in block comment
docs.text:
Use double quotes in Python docstrings.

Before

Python 
'''
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
'''

After

Python 
"""
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
"""

docs.title: Internal error
docs.text:
Use Exception as superclass of custom exceptions. Most applications should not extend Error or Throwable.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinPython 
class PurchaseException extends Error {}

class PurchaseException extends Error {}

class PurchaseException : Error()

class PurchaseException(BaseException):
    pass

After

JavaGroovyKotlinPython 
class PurchaseException extends Exception {}

class PurchaseException extends Exception {}

class PurchaseException : Exception()

class PurchaseException(Exception):
    pass

docs.title: Lowercase d
docs.text:
Double floating point literals should be suffixed with lowercase d, which is more readable than D.

Before

JavaGroovy 
double quarter = 0.25D;

var quarter = 0.25D

After

JavaGroovy 
double quarter = 0.25d;

var quarter = 0.25d

docs.title: Lowercase f
docs.text:
Floating point literals should be suffixed with lowercase f.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++ 
float half = 0.5F;

var quarter = 0.25F

val half = 0.5F

float half = 0.5F;

After

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++ 
float half = 0.5f;

var quarter = 0.25f

val half = 0.5f

float half = 0.5f;

docs.title: Lowercase Hexadecimal
docs.text:
All letters in hexadecimal literals should be lowercase.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
int color = 0xFF00FF;

var color = 0xFF00FF

val color = 0xFF00FF

int color = 0xFF00FF;

color = 0xFF00FF

const color = 0xFF00FF;

const color: number = 0xFF00FF;

After

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
int color = 0xff00ff;

var color = 0xff00ff

val color = 0xff00ff

int color = 0xff00ff;

color = 0xff00ff

docs.title: Lowercase i
docs.text:
Integer literals should be suffixed with lowercase i.

Before

Groovy 
var ten = 10I

After

Groovy 
var ten = 10i

docs.title: Missing inline in contract
docs.text:
Kotlin contract functions that carry a runnable parameter should have inline modifier. Without the modifier, user cannot assign a global variable within the code block.

Before

Kotlin 
fun action(block: () -> Unit) {
    contract { callsInPlace(block, EXACTLY_ONCE) }
    block()
}

After

Kotlin 
inline fun action(block: () -> Unit) {
    contract { callsInPlace(block, EXACTLY_ONCE) }
    block()
}

docs.title: Missing private constructor
docs.text:
Utility classes should have a final modifier and a private constructor to prevent instantiation.

Before

JavaGroovy 
class Lists {
    static List<String> of(String... elements) {
        return Arrays.asList(elements);
    }
}

class Lists {
    static List<String> of(String... elements) {
        return Arrays.asList(elements)
    }
}

After

JavaGroovy 
final class Lists {
    private Lists() {}

    static List<String> of(String... elements) {
        return Arrays.asList(elements);
    }
}

final class Lists {
    private Lists() {}

    static List<String> of(String... elements) {
        return Arrays.asList(elements)
    }
}

docs.title: Redundant qualifier
docs.text:
Strip fully qualified names when they are already imported.

Before

JavaGroovy 
import java.io.FileInputStream;

void read(java.io.FileInputStream stream) {}

import java.io.FileInputStream

def read(java.io.FileInputStream stream) {}

After

JavaGroovy 
import java.io.FileInputStream;

void read(FileInputStream stream) {}

import java.io.FileInputStream

def read(FileInputStream stream) {}

docs.title: Single quotes in literal
docs.text:
Wrap string in single quotes, unless there is a template or a single quote.

Before

GroovyPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
var name = "John Doe"

println('G\'day, ' + name)

name = "John Doe"

print('G\'day, ' + name)

const name = "John Doe";

console.log('G\'day, ' + name);

const name: string = "John Doe";

console.log('G\'day, ' + name);

After

GroovyPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
var name = 'John Doe'

println("G'day, " + name)

name = 'John Doe'

print("G'day, " + name)

const name = 'John Doe';

console.log(`G'day, ${name}`);

const name: string = 'John Doe';

console.log(`G'day, ${name}`);

docs.title: Unnecessary abstract
docs.text:
Abstract classes need at least one abstract function.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonTypeScript 
abstract class Vehicle {
    void start() {}
}

abstract class Vehicle {
    def start() {}
}

abstract class Vehicle {
    fun start() {}
}

from abc import ABC

class Vehicle(ABC):
    def start(self):
        pass

abstract class Vehicle {
    start() {}
}

After

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonTypeScript 
class Vehicle {
    void start() {}
}

class Vehicle {
    def start() {}
}

class Vehicle {
    fun start() {}
}

class Vehicle:
    def start(self):
        pass

class Vehicle {
    start() {}
}

docs.title: Unnecessary parentheses in lambda
docs.text:
Single parameter lambdas should not have parentheses.

Before

JavaGroovyJavaScriptTypeScript 
files.forEach((file) -> System.out.println(file));

files.forEach((file) -> System.out.println(file))

files.forEach((file) => console.log(file));

files.forEach((file: string) => console.log(file));

After

JavaGroovyJavaScriptTypeScript 
files.forEach(file -> System.out.println(file));

files.forEach(file -> System.out.println(file))

files.forEach(file => console.log(file));

files.forEach((file: string) => console.log(file));

Tip

Parentheses on lambda parameters is a syntax error in:

  • Kotlin if parameter is single
  • Groovy closures, not lambdas

docs.title: Uppercase L
docs.text:
Long integer literals should be suffixed with uppercase L.

Before

JavaGroovyC/C++ 
long tenMillion = 10_000_000l;

var tenMillion = 10_000_000l

long ten_million = 10'000'000l;

After

JavaGroovyC/C++ 
long tenMillion = 10_000_000L;

var tenMillion = 10_000_000L

long ten_million = 10'000'000L;

docs.title: Complicated boolean equality
docs.text:
Simplify boolean expressions when applicable.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonTypeScript 
boolean valid = isReady() == true && isRunning() == false;

var valid = isReady() == true && isRunning() == false

val valid = isReady() == true && isRunning() == false

valid = is_ready() == True and is_running() == False

const valid: boolean = isReady() === true && isRunning() === false;

After

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonTypeScript 
boolean valid = isReady() && !isRunning();

var valid = isReady() && !isRunning()

val valid = isReady() && !isRunning()

valid = is_ready() and not is_running()

const valid: boolean = isReady() && !isRunning();

docs.title: Complicated size equality
docs.text:
Use isEmpty() or isNotEmpty() instead of comparing collection size with zero. In languages with truthy values, use the collection itself in a boolean context.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
if (files.size() > 0) {
    collect(files);
}

if (files.size() > 0) {
    collect(files)
}

if (files.size > 0) {
    collect(files)
}

if len(files) > 0:
    collect(files)

if (files.length > 0) {
    collect(files);
}

if (files.length > 0) {
    collect(files);
}

After

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
if (!files.isEmpty()) {
    collect(files);
}

if (!files.isEmpty()) {
    collect(files)
}

if (files.isNotEmpty()) {
    collect(files)
}

if files:
    collect(files)

if (files.length) {
    collect(files);
}

if (files.length) {
    collect(files);
}

docs.title: Confusing predicate
docs.text:
Use the positive form in a predicate call when it is a single expression and the calling function can be inverted.

Before

Kotlin 
person.takeUnless { it.name != null }

After

Kotlin 
person.takeIf { it.name == null }

docs.title: Deprecated identity
docs.text:
Use structural equality instead of referential equality when comparing with primitive values. In Kotlin, null is included in the set of primitive values.

Before

KotlinPython 
user.takeUnless { it.name === null }

if len(user.addresses) is 1:
    continue

After

KotlinPython 
user.takeUnless { it.name == null }

if len(user.addresses) == 1:
    continue

docs.title: Redundant equality
docs.text:
Compare primitives with identity operator ===.

Before

GroovyJavaScriptTypeScript 
if (total == 0) {
    return
}

if (total == 0) {
    return;
}

if (total == 0) {
    return;
}

After

GroovyJavaScriptTypeScript 
if (total === 0) {
    return
}

if (total === 0) {
    return;
}

if (total === 0) {
    return;
}

docs.title: Redundant equals
docs.text:
Use explicit operator instead of named function in Groovy.

Before

Groovy 
if (total.equals(0)) {
    return
}

After

Groovy 
if (total == 0) {
    return
}

docs.title: Empty file
docs.text:
Empty files should be removed. JVM source files with only package declaration is considered empty.

Before

package awesome.company;

package awesome.company

package awesome.company

docs.title: Boolean property interoperability
docs.text:
Kotlin field definitions that are Boolean types should be prefixed with is. Otherwise, the compiler will generate a getter method with get prefix.

Before

After

boolean isActive() {}

    pass

docs.title: Generic name
docs.text:
Generic type parameters should be named with a single uppercase letter. This rule is ignored when there are multiple generic type parameters in the same declaration.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonTypeScript 
class Box<Element> {}

void <Type> rotate(Box<Type> box) {}

class Box<Element> {}

void <Type> rotate(Box<Type> box) {}

class Box<Element>() {}

fun <Type> rotate(box: Box<Type>) {}

template <typename Element>
class Box {};

template <typename Type>
void rotate(Box<Type> box) {}

from typing import TypeVar

Element = TypeVar('Element')
Type = TypeVar('Type')


class Box(Element):
    pass


def rotate(box: Box[Type]):
    pass

class Box<Element> {}

function rotate<Type>(box: Box<Type>): void {}

After

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonTypeScript 
class Box<E> {}

void <T> rotate(Box<T> box) {}

class Box<E> {}

void <T> rotate(Box<T> box) {}

class Box<E>() {}

fun <T> rotate(box: Box<T>) {}

template <typename E>
class Box {};

template <typename T>
void rotate(Box<T> box) {}

from typing import TypeVar

E = TypeVar('E')
T = TypeVar('T')


class Box(E):
    pass


def rotate(box: Box[T]):
    pass

class Box<E> {}

function rotate<T>(box: Box<T>): void {}

docs.title: Identifier name
docs.text:
Non-constant fields, functions and parameters should be written in camelCase. In Python and C/C++, the snake_case style is used instead.

Before

    User AnotherUser = User;
}

}

}

void DebugUser(User User) {
    User AnotherUser = User;
}

def DebugUser(User):
    AnotherUser = User

function DebugUser(User: User): void {
    const AnotherUser: User = User;
}

After

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonTypeScript 
void debugUser(User user) {
    User anotherUser = user;
}

def debugUser(User user) {
    var anotherUser = user
}

fun debugUser(user: User) {
    val anotherUser = user
}

void debug_user(User user) {
    User another_user = user;
}

def debug_user(user):
    another_user = user

function debugUser(user: User): void {
    const anotherUser: User = user;
}

people: list[Person]

docs.title: Common function position
docs.text:
Place Object built-in methods such as toString(), hashCode() and equals() at the end of the class.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinPythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
class Notification {
    private String message;

    public Notification(String message) {
        this.message = message;
    }

    @Override
    public String toString() {
        return message;
    }

    public void notify() {
        return System.out.println(message);
    }
}

class Notification {
    private var message

    public Notification(String message) {
        this.message = message
    }

    @Override
    public def toString() {
        return message
    }

    public def notify() {
        println(message)
    }
}

class Notification(private val message: String) {
    public override fun toString(): String = message

    public fun notify() {
        println(message)
    }
}

class Notification:
    def __init__(self, message):
        self.message = message
        self.id = randomize()

    def __str__(self):
        return self.message

    def notify(self):
        print(self.message)

class Notification {
    constructor(message) {
        this.message = message;
    }

    toString() {
        return this.message;
    }

    notify() {
        console.log(this.message);
    }
}

class Notification {
    constructor(message: string) {
        this.message = message;
    }

    toString(): string {
        return this.message;
    }

    notify(): void {
        console.log(this.message);
    }
}

After

docs.title: Member order
docs.text:
The class should be organized as follows: properties, initializer block, constructors, methods and static members. Inner classes should be declared next to the last member that uses them.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
class Car {
    static void log(String message) {
        System.out.println(message);
    }

    Car(String brand, String model) {}

    Car(String brand) {
        this(brand, "Unknown");
    }

    int wheels = 4;

    void start() {
        log("Car created");
    }
}

class Car {
    static def log(String message) {
        System.out.println(message)
    }

    Car(String brand, String model) {}

    Car(String brand) {
        this(brand, 'Unknown')
    }

    var wheels = 4

    def start() {
        log('Car created')
    }
}

class Car(brand: String, model: String) {
    companion object {
        fun log(message: String) {
            println(message)
        }
    }

    init {
        log("Car created")
    }

    constructor(brand: String) : this(brand, "Unknown")

    val wheels = 4

    fun start() {
        log("Car started")
    }
}

class Car {
    static void log(std::string message) {
        std::cout << message << std::endl;
    }

    Car(std::string brand, std::string model) {}

    Car(std::string brand) : Car(brand, "Unknown") {}

    int wheels = 4;

    void start() {
        log("Car created");
    }
}

class Car:
    @staticmethod
    def log(message: str):
        print(message)

    def __init__(self, brand, model = 'Unknown'):
        pass

    wheels = 4

    def start(self):
        log('Car started')

class Car {
    static log(message) {
        console.log(message);
    }

    constructor(brand, model) {}

    constructor(brand) {
        this(brand, 'Unknown');
    }

    wheels = 4;

    start() {
        Car.log('Car created');
    }
}

class Car {
    static log(message: string): void {
        console.log(message);
    }

    constructor(brand: string, model: string) {}

    constructor(brand: string) {
        this(brand, 'Unknown');
    }

    wheels: number = 4;

    start(): void {
        Car.log('Car created');
    }
}

After

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
class Car {
    int wheels = 4;

    Car(String brand, String model) {}

    Car(String brand) {
        this(brand, "Unknown");
    }

    void start() {

    }

    static void log(String message) {
        System.out.println(message);
    }
}

class Car {
    var wheels = 4

    }

Visibility modifiers first, followed by abstraction modifiers, then other modifiers.

    static final int MAX_RANGE = 100;
}

    static final var MAX_RANGE = 100
}

        const val MAX_RANGE = 100
    }
}

docs.title: Decentralized dependency
docs.text:
Declare dependencies in a centralizeed version catalog file and refer to them by their alias in the build script.

docs.title: Eager API
docs.text:

    println 'Compilation completed'
}

docs.title: Case separator
docs.text:

Before

switch (event) {
    case CANCELLED:

    case PAST:
        String message = "Event is in the past";
        throw new IllegalStateException(message);
    default:
        createEvent(event);
}

    case CANCELLED:
        return

    case PAST:
        var message = 'Event is in the past'
        throw new IllegalStateException(message)
    default:
        createEvent(event)

    event.isCancelled() -> return

    event.date < now -> {
        val message = "Event is in the past"
        throw IllegalStateException(message)
    }
    else -> createEvent(event)
}

    case CANCELLED:
        return;

    case PAST: {
        std::string message = "Event is in the past";
        throw std::logic_error(message);
    }
    default:
        create_event(event);
}

match event:
    case CANCELLED:
        return

    case PAST:
        message = 'Event is in the past'
        raise ValueError(message)
    case _:
        create_event(event)

    case CANCELLED:
        return;

    case PAST: {
        const message = 'Event is in the past';
        throw new Error(message);
    }
    default:
        createEvent(event);

    start(): void {}

docs.title: Illegal catch
docs.text:
Catch specific exception subclass instead of the generic Throwable, Exception or Error.

Before

JavaGroovyC/C++Python 
try {
    unsafeOperation();
} catch (Throwable e) {
    e.printStackTrace();
}

try {
    unsafeOperation()
} catch (Throwable e) {
    e.printStackTrace()
}

try {
    unsafe_operation();
} catch (std::exception& e) {
    std::cerr << e.what() << std::endl;
}

try:
    unsafe_operation()
except Exception as e:
    print(e)

After

JavaGroovyC/C++Python 
try {
    unsafeOperation();
} catch (IOException | SQLException e) {
    e.printStackTrace();
}

try {
    unsafeOperation()
} catch (IOException | SQLException e) {
    e.printStackTrace()
}

try {
    unsafe_operation();
} catch (std::ios_base::failure& e) {
    std::cerr << e.what() << std::endl;
}

try:
    unsafe_operation()
except (IOError, OSError) as e:
    print(e)

docs.title: Redundant default
docs.text:
If every branch of a switch statement has a continue, return or throw statement, the default branch can be lifted.

Before

JavaGroovyKotlinC/C++PythonJavaScriptTypeScript 
void park(Car car) {
    switch (car) {
        case MOVING: throw new IllegalStateException();
        case PARKED: return;
        default: findParking(car);
    }
}

def park(Car car) {
    switch (car) {
        case MOVING: throw new IllegalStateException()
        case PARKED: return
        default: findParking(car)
    }
}

fun park(car: Car) {
    when {
        car.isMoving() -> throw IllegalStateException()
        car.isParked() -> return
        else -> findParking(car)
    }
}

void park(Car car) {
    switch (car) {
        case MOVING: throw IllegalStateException();
        case PARKED: return;
        default: findParking(car);
    }
}

def park(car):
    match car:
        case Car.MOVING: raise ValueError()
        case Car.PARKED: return
        case _: find_parking(car)

function park(car) {
    switch (car) {
        case MOVING: throw new Error();
        case PARKED: return;
        default: findParking(car);
    }
}

function park(car: Car): void {
    switch (car) {
        case MOVING: throw new Error();
        case PARKED: return;
        default: findParking(car);
    }
}

        case MOVING: throw new IllegalStateException()
        case PARKED: return

    findParking(car)

    findParking(car)

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Note

This section hopes to clarify some ambiguous terms used in rule names and messages.

Rule names are in form of state (missing-braces) or object (package-name). They are grouped by the intended objective (spacing) rather than the target site (imports). The rule messages that are printed to the console are in the form of verb phrase (Add missing braces or Rename package).

When possible, merge rules of relatable targets that achieve the same goal. For example, missing-if-braces and missing-for-braces can be merged into control-flow-bracing. Ignore this guideline if one of the rules is not applicable by the linter tool or in the language.

docs.title: Class
docs.text:
In this library, a class means a class-like structure, which can be:

  • Class
  • Interface
  • Enum
  • Java-specific constructs
  • Annotation
  • Record (14+)
  • Pattern (17+)
  • Kotlin-specific constructs
  • Data class
  • Sealed class
  • Object

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: Declaring that a class, function or property exists. The implementation details are considered to be definition.

docs.title: Definition
docs.text: Define how a class, function or property is implemented. Because of this, a definition is a more complete description than a declaration.

docs.title: Expression
docs.text: A combination of variables, constants, operators, and functions that are evaluated to produce a value.

docs.title: Function
docs.text: Also referred to as method.

docs.title: Identifier
docs.text: A unique name given to a class, function, or variable.

docs.title: Property
docs.text: Also referred to as field.

docs.title: Statement
docs.text: A complete unit of execution that performs an action. Examples include if statements and for loops. However, variable declarations are grouped under declarations instead of statements.

docs.title: Variable
docs.text: A variable is a local-scoped property in most programming languages. However, in this library, a variable refers to a property and parameter.

docs.title: Add
docs.text: Insert a new component to the codebase.

docs.title: Arrange
docs.text: Reorganize the order of components.

docs.title: Omit
docs.text: Exclude certain elements without altering the rest of the component.

docs.title: Put
docs.text: Include certain elements in a component without altering the rest of the component.

docs.title: Move
docs.text: Change the position of a component.

docs.title: Remove
docs.text: Take out the whole component from the codebase.

docs.title: Equality
docs.text: Structural, or value-based comparison.

docs.title: Identity
docs.text: Reference-based comparison.
